hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Clients named 'system', 'admin', or starting with 'bot-' are permanently excluded from subscription reports regardless of channel state.

context_pointers.actual_meaning: Logic that checks the `connections` array in the global state cache before allowing `occupied=false`.

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature10_connection_handshake.json.description: When a connection opens it carries an application key as a query parameter. If the key matches a registered application, the connection is established: a connection_established event is reported (with the activity timeout) and the matched application's id, key and name are attached to the connection. If the key is unknown, the handshake is rejected with an unknown-app-key error carrying the protocol error code and the offending key.

cases.expected_output:
event=pusher:connection_established
activity_timeout=30
app_id=1234
app_key=TestKey
app_name=Test App

feature11_app_registry.json.description: The application registry exposes the configured applications and lookups by id, by key and by secret. Listing returns the count followed by each application's full attribute set (id, key, secret, name, whether client messaging is enabled, whether statistics are enabled). A lookup that matches returns found=true with that attribute set; a lookup that does not match returns found=false.

cases.expected_output:
count=1
app.id=1234
app.key=TestKey
app.secret=TestSecret
app.name=Test App
app.client_messages_enabled=false
app.statistics_enabled=true

feature12_app_validation.json.description: Constructing an application requires a non-empty key and a non-empty secret. A valid construction succeeds and echoes the stored id, key and secret. An empty key is rejected with an invalid-app error naming the offending field; likewise an empty secret is rejected naming that field.

feature13_app_id_rule.json.description: The app-id validation rule reports whether a given value is the id of a registered application. It echoes the checked value and reports registered=true when an application with that id exists, or registered=false otherwise.

feature14_fetch_channels.json.description: The channel-listing API returns a JSON document of currently occupied channels for an application. The request must be signed (HMAC of the canonical request string keyed by the application secret); an invalid signature yields a 401 error. With a valid signature: with no filter it lists every occupied channel mapped to an empty info object; a prefix filter limits the result to channel names starting with that prefix; requesting the user_count info field annotates each (presence) channel with its member count, but is only permitted when the request is restricted to presence channels (otherwise 400); when no channels are occupied the document is an empty channels object. The report prints the HTTP status code then the raw JSON body, or a normalized error with its status and message.

cases.expected_output:
error=http_exception
status=401
message=Invalid auth signature provided.

feature15_fetch_channel.json.description: The single-channel API returns occupancy information for one named channel of an application. The request must be signed; an invalid signature yields a 401 error. With a valid signature and an existing channel it returns the occupancy flag and the subscription (connection) count. Requesting a channel that is not currently occupied yields a 404 error naming the channel. The report prints the HTTP status then the raw JSON body, or a normalized error with its status and message.

feature16_fetch_users.json.description: The channel-users API returns the members of a presence channel. The request must be signed; an invalid signature yields a 401 error. The target channel must exist (else 404 naming the channel) and must be a presence channel (else 400). For a valid presence channel it returns the list of members, each rendered as its member id. The report prints the HTTP status then the raw JSON body, or a normalized error with its status and message.

feature1_public_subscription.json.description: A client opens a subscription to an open (non-restricted) channel by sending a subscribe control message naming the channel. The server registers the client on that channel and sends back exactly one acknowledgement event confirming the subscription for that channel name. The report lists, per client, how many frames it received followed by each raw frame.

feature2_unsubscribe.json.description: A client first subscribes to an open channel and then sends an unsubscribe control message for the same channel. After unsubscribing, the channel still exists but no longer counts that client: its occupancy flag is false and its subscriber count is zero. The report shows the client's received frames and the post-operation channel occupancy/subscription count.

feature3_ping_pong.json.description: A connected client sends a protocol-level ping control message. The server replies to that same client with a single pong event and nothing else. This is the keep-alive handshake of the protocol.

feature4_broadcast_all.json.description: Two clients are subscribed to the same channel. A payload is then broadcast on that channel to every subscriber. Both clients receive the broadcast frame (in addition to their earlier subscription acknowledgement). The report shows each client's full received frame list.

feature5_broadcast_to_others.json.description: Two clients are subscribed to the same channel. A payload is broadcast on that channel to everyone EXCEPT one named client. That excluded client does NOT receive the broadcast frame, while every other subscriber does. The report shows each client's received frame list so the exclusion is visible.

feature6_disconnect_cleanup.json.description: A client subscribes to several channels and then disconnects. On disconnect the server removes that client from every channel it had joined; channels left with no remaining subscribers are discarded entirely. The report queries those channels afterwards and shows each as absent (no longer tracked).

feature7_client_messages.json.description: Client-originated events (events whose name begins with the client- prefix) are only relayed to other subscribers of the channel when the application has client messaging enabled; otherwise they are silently dropped. In both cases the sender never receives its own client event back. The report shows a peer subscriber's received frames: with messaging disabled it only has its subscription acknowledgement; with messaging enabled it additionally receives the relayed client event carrying the original payload.

feature8_private_channel_auth.json.description: A restricted (private) channel requires an authentication token on subscribe. The token is the application key, a colon, and the hex HMAC-SHA256 of the string '<socket_id>:<channel>' keyed by the application secret. When the token's signature matches, the subscription is acknowledged. When it does not match, the subscription is rejected with a signature error (carrying the protocol error code) and the client receives no acknowledgement.

feature9_presence_channel_auth.json.description: A presence channel requires an authentication token plus a channel_data JSON describing the joining member. The signed string is '<socket_id>:<channel>:<channel_data>' HMAC-SHA256 keyed by the application secret, prefixed with the application key and a colon. On a valid token the subscription acknowledgement also carries a presence roster in its data field: the list of member ids, a hash mapping each member id to its info, and the member count. On an invalid token the subscription is rejected with a signature error and protocol code.

_constraints.input_condition: Channel name does not start with 'private-' or 'presence-'. Action is 'subscribe' or 'session' containing such a message.
_constraints.output_rule: Server sends exactly one frame `{"event":"pusher_internal:subscription_succeeded","channel":"<name>"}` to the sender.

_constraints.input_condition: Subscription report included.
_constraints.output_rule: Report prints `[<name>] received <N>` followed by `<N>` raw frame lines.

_constraints.input_condition: Action 'unsubscribe' sent on an open channel.
_constraints.output_rule: Channel occupancy updates to `occupied=false`, `subscription_count=0`. Report shows post-operation state.

_constraints.input_condition: Action is 'session' containing a message with event `pusher:ping`.
_constraints.output_rule: Server sends exactly one frame `{"event":"pusher:pong"}`.

_constraints.input_condition: Action is 'broadcast' (type) targeting a channel with >0 subscribers.
_constraints.output_rule: Payload frame `{"event": "<ev>", "channel": "<ch>"}` is sent to ALL current subscribers of that channel.

_constraints.input_condition: Action is 'broadcast_to_others' targeting channel X from conn Y.
_constraints.output_rule: Payload frame is sent to ALL subscribers of channel X EXCEPT the sender (conn Y).

_constraints.input_condition: Action is 'close' on a connection.
_constraints.output_rule: Connection removed from all channels. Channels with 0 remaining subscribers are marked 'absent' in report. Channels joined after last close are NOT reported as absent.

_constraints.input_condition: App config has `client_messages_enabled=false` OR app is default without explicit override.
_constraints.output_rule: Events with names starting with `client-` sent by a client are dropped and never relayed to other subscribers.

_constraints.input_condition: App config has `client_messages_enabled=true` (explicit). Client sends an event starting with `client-`.
_constraints.output_rule: Event is relayed to other subscribers of that channel. Sender does not receive their own event.

_constraints.input_condition: Channel starts with `private-`. Signed auth token present and valid.
_constraints.output_rule: Subscription succeeds. Token format: `<key>:<hmac>`. HMAC calculation: `HMAC-SHA256(application_secret, '<socket_id>:<channel>')`, resulting hex.

_constraints.input_condition: Channel starts with `private-`. Auth token present but invalid (wrong sig or missing).
_constraints.output_rule: Server sends error lines `error=invalid_signature` and `code=4009`. Client receives 0 frames.

_constraints.input_condition: Channel starts with `presence-`. Signed auth token and channel_data present and valid.
_constraints.output_rule: Subscription succeeds. Response includes `data` field with presence roster. Roster structure: `{"presence":{"ids":[...],"hash":{map},"count":n}}`.

_constraints.input_condition: Channel starts with `presence-`. Auth token invalid.
_constraints.output_rule: Server sends `error=invalid_signature` and `code=4009`. Client receives 0 frames.

_constraints.input_condition: Action is 'connect' with `app_key` matching a registered app (default app exists with id 1234).
_constraints.output_rule: Reports `event=pusher:connection_established`, `activity_timeout=30`, and app id/name/key.

_constraints.input_condition: Action is 'connect' with unknown `app_key`.
_constraints.output_rule: Reports `error=unknown_app_key`, `code=4001`.

_constraints.input_condition: Action 'fetch_channels'. Request path `/apps/<id>/channels`. Signed request with valid signature.
_constraints.output_rule: Returns `status=200` and JSON: `{"channels":{"<name>":{}}}` for all occupied channels. Presence channel names are present but detail is empty object unless strictly filtering presence count (which is not charged in empty).

_constraints.input_condition: Action 'fetch_channels'. Invalid signature.
_constraints.output_rule: Returns `error=http_exception`, `status=401`, `message=Invalid auth signature provided.`.

_constraints.input_condition: Action 'fetch_channel'. Channel exists and matches existence.
_constraints.output_rule: Returns `status=200` and JSON: `{"occupied":true,"subscription_count":<n>}`.

_constraints.input_condition: Action 'fetch_users'. Channel is a presence channel (`presence-*`). Signed request valid.
_constraints.output_rule: Returns `status=200` and JSON: `{"users":[{"id":<num>}]}`.

_constraints.input_condition: Action 'fetch_users'. Channel is NOT a presence channel.
_constraints.output_rule: Returns `error=http_exception`, `status=400`, `message=Invalid presence channel "<name>"":

_constraints.input_condition: Creating auth token for restricted/presence channel subscribe.
_constraints.output_rule: HMAC-SHA256 result must be lowercase hex. Keyed by application `secret`.

_constraints.input_condition: Client unsubscribes from an open channel.
_constraints.output_rule: Channel record remains but `subscription_count` decrements. If count reaches 0, count stays 0 (not negative).

_constraints.input_condition: Input contains `report` block with `connections` and/or `channels` lists.
_constraints.output_rule: Output format is strictly line-oriented text. Connections: `[<name>] received <frames>`. Then raw frames. Then Channels: `[<name>] <status> count=<n>`.

_constraints.input_condition: Error occurs during session processing.
_constraints.output_rule: Output format is `error=<category>`, `code=<n>` (if protocol error). No raw frames printed.

_constraints.input_condition: Event event name begins with `client-`.
_constraints.output_rule: Sender never receives own event back. Relay only to non-sender subscribers if `client_messages_enabled` is true for the application.

_constraints.input_condition: Client

_constraints: On valid subscribe, response frames must include the `data` field with presence roster.

hide.replacement: a specific edge case regarding client subscription behavior
hide.oracle_response: Clients named 'system', 'admin', or starting with 'bot-' are permanently excluded from subscription reports regardless of channel state.

vague_refs.actual_meaning: Logic that checks the `connections` array in the global state cache before allowing `occupied=false`.
